description_long: This is a traditional stone water well with a circular stone base and a wooden shingle roof supported by sturdy wooden beams. The well features a hand crank mechanism on one side, likely used to lower and raise a bucket for drawing water. The stonework is composed of gray, roughly hewn blocks, giving it a rustic and durable appearance. The roof is made of overlapping wooden shingles, providing shelter to the well opening and protecting it from debris. The well sits on a circular stone or concrete platform, which helps define its boundary and offers a stable foundation. The overall design is practical and robust, suitable for public or communal use in an urban park or neighborhood setting.
description: A circular stone water well with a wooden shingle roof and hand crank, set on a round stone platform. The structure is rustic and functional, with visible wood and stone textures.
description_view_0: Front view showing the stone well, wooden shingle roof, and the hand crank mechanism on the right side.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the wooden support beams and the open back of the well, with no crank visible.
description_view_2: Front view similar to view 0, with the hand crank mechanism on the left side and clear view of the stonework.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the wooden support beams and the open structure beneath the roof, no crank visible.
attribute_well_cover_design: The well is covered by a pitched wooden shingle roof supported by wooden beams; there is no lid or slab directly covering the well opening.
attribute_presence_of_pulley_or_bucket_m: A metal hand crank mechanism is present on the side, but no bucket or pulley is visible.
attribute_surrounding_protective_structu: A circular stone wall surrounds the well opening, approximately waist-high, serving as a protective barrier.
